questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: 6 gm of magnesium $(\mathrm{Mg})$ is burnt with insufficient amount of oxygen. The residue is treated with 100 ml of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution ( $30 \%$ by mass, $1.4 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{ml}$ density), resulting in an evolution of 3.36 litre of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas at STP. After the reaction, density of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution is found to be $1.25 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{ml}$. Assume no change in volume of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution.Question: Percentage $w / w$ of final $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution is :
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: 6 gm of magnesium $(\mathrm{Mg})$ is burnt with insufficient amount of oxygen. The residue is treated with 100 ml of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution ( $30 \%$ by mass, $1.4 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{ml}$ density), resulting in an evolution of 3.36 litre of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas at STP. After the reaction, density of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution is found to be $1.25 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{ml}$. Assume no change in volume of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution.Question: Mass of oxygen used is :
